text1: A crewed cataphract frame recovered from a derelict research habitat, grown around a fabricator lattice rather than assembled. The lattice re-grows the hull to suit its pilot, and no two certified configurations weigh, handle, or scan alike. The research cell named each one for a blade out of Old Earth legend. Nothing in the surviving logs says what the lattice itself was grown from.

The first configuration the research cell certified, and the pattern for everything after it. Durendal anchors a line, holds ground other ships would abandon, and teaches its pilots calm. The name belongs to a sword that could not be broken.
Hauteclere is the duelist's cut of Durendal, rebuilt for pilots who would rather answer a blow than outlast it. The configuration has heavy demands of its pilots.

The emperor's blade stood for peace, which the research cell read as an instruction about fortification. Joyeuse is the escort configuration: it puts itself between the convoy and the guns. Crews standing behind one learn to trust its defenses.

Longinus shoots from outside the battle. The configuration trades armor, speed, and every close-in consideration for reach, and its fire arrives without warning or apparent origin.

Every mounted weapon has 3x range.

Named for a spear that killed whenever it was thrown, shots from Gae Bolg are decisive. It destroys fortified positions in single strikes, as long as those shots hit.

Every weapon deals 3x damage and has 2x range, but consumes double ammo and locks for 5 seconds after it fires. If there is insufficient ammo, the frame takes half of the weapon's damage. Destroying a ship removes the lock.

The king's lance was a pillar before it was a weapon. Rhongomyniad is the siege configuration: it plants itself, aims at a single mark, and trusts its escorts to handle defense.

Every weapon has 2x range. Its arm is a continuous beam that ramps up to 4x damage over 6 seconds on a single target. Your top speed drops to 5% while it fires, and switching targets resets the ramp.

Curtana carries the sword of mercy, the blade with its point struck off, an edge that hardly cuts at all. Certified doctrine is to close, touch, and hold.

As long as its arm beam is hitting a ship's hull, that ship is slowed to 50% timeflow.

Clarent fights like Curtana with the mercy removed, and it returns from trials in better repair than it left. Targets are frozen in time instead of merely slowed.

As long as its arm beam is hitting a ship's hull, the beam's target is frozen, you vent flux twice as fast, weapon cooldowns are decreased by 50%, and you drain 15 hull per second from the target.

Fragarach is the squadron configuration. Wings flying with one are a half-step ahead of every exchange, and their sortie plans carry noticeably thin contingency annexes. Old Earth remembered the blade as the Answerer.

The beam becomes a radial pulse. When fired, enemy units within 800 units are slowed by 35% for 4 seconds, and allies are sped up.
Throws the frame a short distance along its facing, the lattice re-weaves the hull around the jump rather than pushing it through.
Marks a single target. The target takes increased damage from all sources.

Instantly raises a barrier that while active blocks all damage with no flux cost per hit. 50% of any blocked damage returns to the attacker as an EMP arc. Generates 900 hard flux per second, and has a two-second cooldown between activations.

Instantly raises a barrier that while active blocks all damage with no flux cost per hit. Hits blocked within the first 0.15 seconds reflect 200% damage and skip the ward's next cooldown. Later blocks reflect 25%. Generates 900 hard flux per second, and has a two-second cooldown between activations.

Instantly raises a barrier that while active blocks all damage with no flux cost per hit. 50% of any blocked damage returns to the attacker as an EMP arc, 20% of blocked damage repairs your hull, and 5% repairs your armor. Your top speed decreases to 10% while active. Generates 900 hard flux per second, and has a two-second cooldown between activations.
A plain kinetic autocannon the lattice grew from its own stores when asked for a weapon.
A forearm ram carrying a short-throw concussive emitter.
The bastion gauntlet lightened and edged. It trades throw weight for cycle time, on the theory that the pilot will not miss twice.
A gauntlet fused into a tower-shield slab; the emitter fires through the shield face.
A precision kinetic rifle grown around a lattice waveguide. Its rounds hold their line far past any rated engagement envelope.
A heavy launcher-lance. Each firing expends the whole weapon, and the lattice grows the next round in place.
A beam lance the frame is designed around. Output climbs the longer it has a consistent target.
A blade-format beam that barely scratches a hull. Instead, it speeds up time for the target, making the rest of the world flash by.
A shorter, hungrier weave of the mercy edge. While its target is frozen, the frame vents heat down the blade's thread and pulls material back up it.
A bladeless hilt that casts the freeze outward as a pulse: hostiles are slowed, friendlies are accelerated.
A grown-shut housing where a missile cell should be. The lattice keeps it sealed until the weave unseals it.
